author: parthsingh2006
content:
hey everyone , Parth this side , I am signaling for the `Reth Partial Statelessness Prototype`
me and my friend <@1335660981019480075>  have been going in detail about the project since the list came out
I think its the best fit because it not only aligns with interest but also with the stack

I have already updated my week0 updates: https://hackmd.io/@ParthSinghPS/HJMWUn6xzg
and am actively working on my week1 currently. 

Now to address the elephant in the room, i think this is going to be the most claimed project so I think its best that all those who are interested should share our experiences and expertise so we can divide the work in the later stages as the scope is massive. Its best that we come up with a solution by ourselves and not bother mentors about this.

author: 0xchronos
content: Hello guys, I’ll most probably be working on pureth’s project idea, starting from log reform. I mainly work in rust so im exploring rust based ssz implementation right and still taking my time understanding  the whole eip 7745 and szz.

author: astrion0587
content: completely agreed on the not bothering mentors part, even though I think its better if we once get clarification from <@686695036733227017> and <@914307919699406908> if they are ok with this many people proceeding with one project because in the kick off call, I recall them mentioning that they generally don't recommend more than 3 people working on one project, what do you think? If some of us have to change the project, its better to do it early on as opposed to later right?

author: tmiychao
content: This is project signaling thread is meant to serve for upcoming weeks after you do some research and talk to people. It's way to early to commit to any project without understanding its scope. I am glad to see some productive discussion here but please take your time and research implications of the project, its scope and what it actually means. Project signaling is meant to connect you if you are interested in similar areas so you can talk to each other, do a call and coordinate, not to 'claim' any project

author: tmiychao
content: There is not enough projects proposed by client teams for everyone but the there is infinite amount of work on the protocol. There is a lot of space to propose your own ideas.

author: tmiychao
content:
This Phase 1 exploration is about checking all projects and diving into subset of them, researching multiple options. Maybe we opened this thread too soon and gave you a wrong idea, sorry huys.
Honestly no idea why reth statelessness is so popular because it's just a prototype of an idea that is very probably not going to be long term solution in the client and reth has already many contributors with little space to work with them or hire. I mean, it's a cool project but seems like people don't know what they are getting themselves into, that's why you need to take time to consider all options

author: drklaus7
content: Hey everyone, Ritesh this side. I'll be working on pureth for Nethermind specifically. I have already made some contributions recently to Nethermind on SSZ related stuff so I do have some familiarity with the ssz lib structure in Nethermind codebase and will continue my research.
